description: RENOVATION AND ADDITION OF AN EXISTING 3-STORY BUILDING INTO A 6037SQFT MIXED USE (LEVEL 3 ALTERATION) HOTEL WITH A RESTAURANT AND BAR. NEW FRAMING, FINISHES, STAIRWAYS, ELEVATOR, MECHANICAL, ELETRICAL, & PLUMBING THROUGHOUT. BUILDING TO BE SPRINKLERED AND ALARMED. PAINT ENTIRE INTERIOR AND EXTERIOR, EXCEPT AT EXISTING EXPOSED BRICK. NEW ROOF THROUGHOUT WITH NEW SKYLIGHTS. ALL WORK TO BE DONE AS PER PLANS. ****THE KITCHEN HOOD AND FIRE SUPRESSION SYSTEM IS BEING REQUESTED AS A DEFERRED SUBMITTAL. NO INSTALLATION SHALL BE DONE PRIOR TO PERMITTING.**** Permit extended to July 9, 2027

This project modernizes an existing gymnasium, cafeteria, and support areas to improve overall functionality and accessibility. The core of the work involves interior renovations, including surface finishes, upgraded utility systems, and the installation of a new ADA-compliant elevator. Only minor exterior modifications and selective demolition are required to accommodate the new layout and structural changes.

Renovation of an existing office space on floors 2, 6-9 for Veterans Benefits Administration. They occupy multiply floors in this building. All floors will receive new finishes, lighting, and data cabling. Some areas shall be reconfigured with new partitions. A new Training room with two Motorized partitions shall be installed on the 8th floor. New plumbing shall be installed at new break rooms and wellness rooms. HVAC work shall be limited to the rerouting of supply and return ductwork due to construction. New access control system shall be installed through out.

Renovation of an existing warehouse building into a warehouse and a brewery as per plans. This permit is for Phase I of a 2 Phase renovation. PHASE I WILL INCLUDE RENOVATIONS INTO A BREWERY (PRODUCING FEWER THAN 12,500 BARRELS / YEAR), STORAGE, AND BUSINESS USES, AS WELL AS ROUGH-INS AND FOUNDATIONS FOR LATER PHASES. PHASE II, WHICH WILL BE FILED UNDER A SEPARATE PERMIT, WILL INCLUDE THE DISTILLERY, TASTING ROOM, OUTDOOR SEATING, AND MEZZANINE STRUCTURE. CZO compliance for use and parking for Phase II will be evaluated at that time

description: SHELL/ROOF RENOVATION OF EXISTING RESTAURANT (60:40 KITCHEN:SEATING). PROJECT INCLUDES SELECTIVE DEMOLITION OF PORTIONS OF REST. ROOF & ALL INTERIORS, MEP EQUIP. & STANDALONE PAVILION. FINISHES INCLUDE NEW ROOF & ROOF STRUCTURE, NEW COATINGS ON INTERIOR STRUCTURE & EXISTING EXTERIOR STOREFRONT. NOT FOR OCCUPANCY - INTERIOR WORK ON LATER PERMIT.

FOR ARC REVIEW. New construction of a 3,975 sf building on the corner of St. Thomas and Thalia Street in the Lower Garden District. The plans propose two commercial tenants on the ground floor and two residential tenants upstairs that are accessed via an exterior stair. Horizontal box rib panels are proposed for the upper level. The lower level is indicated as fiber cement siding with mitered corners in lieu of trim.
